steps.title: AIG: f = a·(b+c+d+e), 7 ANDs, depth 4
steps.narrative:
Source: refactor_win.blif writes f the literal way: four product terms ab, ac, ad, ae ORed together as a left-leaning chain t1 = ab+ac, t2 = t1+ad, f = t2+ae. After strash, that's 7 ANDs at depth 4. Dashed edges are negated (the OR encoding uses ¬(¬x · ¬y)).

Rewrite would miss the algebraic factoring here — the cone has 5 inputs, more than rewrite's 4-cut limit. refactor uses a larger reconvergence-driven cut and rebuilds the local function from its truth table.

panes.title: Per-node pipeline
panes.body:
1. reconvergence cut — up to 10 leaves (nNodeSizeMax)
  2. truth-table simulate the cone
  3. Kit_TruthToGraph: ISOP → algebraic factoring → Dec_Graph_t
  4. score with Dec_GraphToNetworkCount; commit if gain > 0

steps.title: Reconvergence-driven cut at f → leaves {a, b, c, d, e}
steps.narrative:
Abc_NodeFindCut(pManCut, f, 0) grows leaves greedily from f, biased toward leaves whose addition increases reconvergence with already-present nodes. With nNodeSizeMax = 10 there's room for all five PIs, so the cut reaches the inputs.

Cone interior: {ab, ac, ad, ae, t1, t2, f} — 7 internal ANDs. Since this is the only fanout chain to the PO and no internal node has external fanouts, the entire cone is the MFFC.

steps.title: Compute the cone's truth table
steps.narrative:
Abc_NodeConeTruth seeds the leaves with the standard 5-input simulation patterns and walks the cone in topological order, ANDing word-by-word. With ≤6 inputs, one 64-bit word suffices: 32 patterns laid out as bits 0..31 (LSB = pattern 0).

The result is f's truth table: 32 bits with a 1 wherever a=1 AND any of b,c,d,e is 1.

steps.title: Kit_TruthToGraph — ISOP, then algebraic factor
steps.narrative:
First an ISOP (irredundant sum-of-products) is extracted from the truth table; for f it's the four single-cube products {ab, ac, ad, ae}. Then algebraic factoring runs common-cube extraction: every cube contains literal a, so a divides out cleanly, leaving the kernel (b+c+d+e).

The resulting Dec_Graph_t has 4 internal nodes — one for each product/OR step in the factored expression. Same algorithm SIS used.

panes.body: FF in the trace = the factored-form node count returned by Dec_GraphNodeNum.

steps.title: Materialize as AIG — Dec_GraphToNetworkCount
steps.narrative:
The Dec_Graph is bottom-up walked through Abc_AigAndLookup: each AND is structurally hashed; an existing match counts as 0, a new AND counts as 1. The 4-input OR is materialized as a balanced binary AND-tree of negated inputs (right pane).

None of (¬b·¬c), (¬d·¬e), (¬b·¬c · ¬d·¬e), or (a · ¬that) exists yet → all 4 ANDs are new. nNodesAdded = 4, Save = 7, gain = 3.

steps.title: Commit — 7 ANDs → 4 ANDs, depth 4 → 3
steps.narrative:
Dec_GraphUpdateNetwork instantiates the 4 new ANDs and redirects f's PO to the new top node. The 7 old MFFC ANDs become dangling and are reclaimed.

The actual ABC trace shows three smaller moves rather than one big one — refactor visits each internal AND in topological order, so it accepts gain=1 at t1 first, then gain=1 at t2 on the updated graph, then gain=1 at f. Cumulative outcome is identical: 7 → 4 ANDs, depth 4 → 3.
